pattern: Only three concatenation lengths matter, since beyond two copies any extra copies just contribute full-array sums when that total is positive.
hints: Repeating the array many times never needs more than two copies of structure plus a stack of full passes; reason about k=1, k=2, and the role of the whole-array sum separately.
Compute the best subarray sum on one copy and on two copies with Kadane; if the total array sum is positive, extra middle copies each add that total.
Answer is max of: best on one copy, and best on two copies plus max(0, (k-2)) times the array total when k>=2; take it modulo 1e9+7 but never negative.

edge_inputs.description: mixed signs, gain only from spanning two copies
all negative means empty subarray wins with sum zero
negative total but a strong internal run

stress.description: moderate array and varied k, small enough for the O(n*k capped) oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    arr = [rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: oracle materializes min(k,3) copies so k is kept small

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def kConcatenationMaxSum(self, arr, k):
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        copies = min(k, 3)
        big = arr * copies
        best = 0
        cur = 0
        for x in big:
            cur = max(0, cur + x)
            best = max(best, cur)
        total = sum(arr)
        if k > 3 and total > 0:
            best += (k - 3) * total
        return best % MOD
